docs.name: collections-zone
headings: The Immutability Constraint — and Why It Forces a Pattern
Sidecar .meta.md — When the Row Isn't Enough
Naming Convention — Soft, Scanned, Not Enforced
Bootstrap behavior on bcos init in a new repo
docs.first_paragraph: How docs/_collections/ works in CLEAR Context OS — what it holds, why files there are immutable, how the manifest pattern makes binary cargo first-class evidence, how its vocabularies grow with the business, and how it stays distinct from _wiki/, _inbox/, and active data points.

docs.name: component-standards
docs.first_paragraph: How to add a new skill, agent, or hook to the CLEAR Context OS ecosystem.

docs.name: content-routing
docs.first_paragraph: How new content enters, flows through, and settles into the right location in CLEAR Context OS. This document covers the mechanics -- for the business rationale behind folder zones and ownership, see docs/_bcos-framework/architecture/system-design.md.

docs.name: context-routing
headings: Source-of-Truth Ranking — How Conflict Resolution Works
How Routing Composes With the Rest of the Architecture
docs.first_paragraph: How CLEAR Context OS turns "I'm about to do task T" into a curated, freshness-flagged, source-of-truth-aware bundle of context. Routing is a derived layer on top of context-zones.md (zones), metadata-system.md (per-doc metadata), and the typed-edge graph in context-index.json (builds-on, references, depends-on, provides).

docs.name: context-zones
headings: How Zones Compose with Other Architecture
docs.first_paragraph: How CLEAR Context OS classifies every file under docs/ into a **zone** — a typed region with its own trust level, freshness model, and source-of-truth role. The zone is what tells the framework whether a doc is canonical truth, derived explanation, frozen evidence, future intent, or historical record.

docs.name: maintenance-lifecycle
headings: Maintenance Lifecycle -- How BCOS Stays Healthy
docs.first_paragraph: Context dies from neglect, drift, accumulation, ownership vacuums, and change blindness. The maintenance lifecycle is the defense system: layered checks at increasing time horizons that catch problems before they compound.

docs.name: metadata-system
docs.first_paragraph: Every managed document in CLEAR Context OS carries YAML frontmatter metadata. This metadata enables automated health checks (hooks and scripts), discovery (Document Index generation), and enforcement (context-audit). This is the single reference for all validation rules.

docs.name: system-design
headings: Tier 1 -- Foundation (always active or frequently invoked)
What Stays in the Main Window
docs.first_paragraph: CLEAR Context OS (BCOS) is a framework that turns a Git repository into a living knowledge base managed by Claude Code. It layers a skill/agent/hook system on top of structured Markdown documents so that organizational knowledge stays accurate, bounded, and discoverable across sessions. The architecture separates *what the business knows* (docs/) from *how that knowledge is maintained* (.claude/)…

docs.name: wiki-zone
headings: Where Wiki Sits Among the Zones
Path A — URL via queue (pin-llm-wiki native)
Path B — Local content (covers _inbox/ promotions AND direct uploads)
The HTML-Comment DSL on queue.md (Path A lifecycle)
What We Adopt as-is from pin-llm-wiki
Bootstrap behavior on bcos init in a new repo
docs.first_paragraph: How docs/_wiki/ works in CLEAR Context OS — what it holds, how content gets in, how it stays healthy, how its vocabularies grow as the business grows, and how it stays distinct from the other zones (data points, collections, inbox, planned, archive). Designed to be AI-friendly today and graph/RAG-ready tomorrow.

docs.name: Collections Schema Governance Implementation Plan
docs.domain_statement: Phased implementation plan for collections schema governance.
exclusively_owns: Maintenance classification for collection files and sidecars.
Test coverage proving collection evidence does not behave like stale wiki content.
strictly_avoids: Re-analyzing artifact content in maintenance unless explicitly requested.
headings: Phase 7: Documentation, Migration, and Release Gate
docs.first_paragraph: Goal: Lock the semantics in tests before implementation.

docs.name: Collections Schema Governance Pre-Flight Decisions
docs.domain_statement: Design decisions required before implementing collections schema governance.
strictly_avoids: Treating collection evidence as wiki pages.
Adding LLM content analysis to routine maintenance.
Requiring every binary or external export to contain frontmatter.
headings: D-01: Collections reuse wiki governance discipline, not wiki semantics
D-02: Artifact lifecycle is separate from status
D-04: Staleness applies only where the policy permits it
D-05: Artifact identity is content-addressed when practical
D-06: Metadata is updateable even when evidence is frozen
D-07: Dashboard and Galaxy visibility are policy-driven
D-08: Routine maintenance does not re-analyze evidence content
D-09: Manifest rows are the schema surface
docs.first_paragraph: Decision: Build collections schema governance with the same discipline as the wiki schema registry: explicit fields, validation, migrations, tests, and command support.

docs.name: Collections Schema Governance Plan
docs.domain_statement: Governance model, schema fields, validation behavior, and maintenance semantics for docs/_collections/.
exclusively_owns: Collection artifact lifecycle classes and maintenance policies.
Rules for when collection entries can be updated, replaced, re-derived, or left frozen.
Dashboard/Galaxy freshness semantics for collection artifacts.
strictly_avoids: Reclassifying collection evidence as current business context.
Building new public UI features before the schema and tests exist.
Re-analyzing evidence content during routine maintenance by default.
docs.first_paragraph: Plan status: awaiting_approval.

headings: D-02 — Collections: real zone vs saved-query view
D-04 — Task profile location: framework vs per-repo
D-05 — Profile granularity: concrete slugs vs patterns
docs.first_paragraph: 10 design decisions the planner defaulted before producing the implementation plan. Approved at Gate 2 (2026-05-04 16:05 UTC). The pre-implementation cleanup pass (2026-05-04 18:25 UTC) tightened **D-10** to remove an auto-trigger LLM path that had crept into P1's task description — see the D-10 entry below.

docs.name: Context Base — Missing Layers (search, sub-agent isolation, stub repair, task-driven routing, embeddings)

meta.name: Context Base — Missing Layers (search, sub-agent isolation, stub repair, task-driven routing, embeddings)

docs.first_paragraph: Polished proposal for the next layers of BCOS's context base: a cross-zone retrieval surface, sub-agent isolation for big-body work, wiki-zone stub repair, and (architecturally most important) task-driven cross-zone routing. Wiki is one zone among several — this plan operates on the whole context surface, not just _wiki/.

docs.name: Wiki Zone Integration — Pre-Flight Decisions
headings: Part A — Critical Issues from Plan Review
D-02 · Schema dependency cycle in P2
D-03 · Cluster vocabulary has no minting path
D-04 · Reference-format ambiguity (slugs vs paths vs wikilinks)
D-05 · Refresh-must-rediscover vs HEAD-check optimization tension
D-07 · P3_009 is obsolete — replace, don't update
D-08 · P9_003 — fix the integration-audit flag
D-10 · Wiki auto-fix entries must follow the new format
D-11 · Architectural — apply derived-artifact pattern to _wiki/index.md
Out of scope for this doc
docs.first_paragraph: Single approval gate before P2 starts. Each decision below has a recommended default. **Saying "approve all defaults" closes the whole list and unblocks P2.** Deviating is fine — flag the IDs you want to change.
